title: Remote Desktop and Screen-Sharing Setup
text:
Q: How do I set up remote desktop and screen-sharing on my computer?
A: Windows: Step 1: Open the Start menu and type 'Remote Desktop Connection', then select it. Step 2: In the Remote Desktop Connection window, enter the name of the computer you want to connect to and click 'Connect'. Step 3: If prompted, enter your credentials (username and password) to access the remote computer. Step 4: To enable remote access on your computer, go to 'System Properties' by right-clicking 'This PC' and selecting 'Properties', then click 'Remote settings' and check 'Allow remote connections to this computer'. macOS: Step 1: Open 'System Preferences' and select 'Sharing'. Step 2: Check the box next to 'Screen Sharing' to enable it. Step 3: Note the address displayed (e.g., vnc://your-ip-address) for remote access. Step 4: To connect to another Mac, open 'Finder', select 'Go' from the menu, and choose 'Connect to Server', then enter the VNC address and click 'Connect'. Step 5: Enter your credentials if prompted. Ensure that both computers are on the same network or have proper internet access for remote connections.
